ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_03301710 covers mushroom soup as a category; no exact entry for Malaysian-style homemade mushroom soup exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00GJ covers soups and broths broadly; no specific mushroom soup code exists. Closest parent category used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226365003 covers soup as a food concept; no mushroom-specific soup code identified. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of homemade mushroom soup, approximately 250 g, as typically served in a Malaysian coffee shop or café setting.

nutrient_source.title: Mushroom soup, prepared from recipe — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB No. 06037)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for cream of mushroom soup prepared with water (NDB 06037 / FDC 173178) used as the primary nutrient anchor. Values represent a moderately creamy mushroom soup. Malaysian homemade versions may vary in cream content and salt; values are representative of a standard preparation. Per-100g values derived from USDA data for soup prepared with water, which approximates a lighter homemade style.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of soups and mixed vegetable dishes — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate based on vegetable soup GI values
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for homemade mushroom soup was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 38 based on analogous vegetable-based soups and cream soups listed in the University of Sydney GI Database, which report GI values in the range 30–50 for low-carbohydrate, broth-based soups. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (38 × 11.5) / 100 ≈ 4.4, where carbohydrate per 250 g serving = 4.6 × 2.5 = 11.5 g.

hypertension.guidance: Homemade mushroom soup contains approximately 397 mg sodium per 100 g (approximately 993 mg per 250 g bowl), which represents a significant portion of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive individuals; patients should request reduced-salt preparation or limit portion size to half a bowl.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and low carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated GI of 38 and approximately 11.5 g of carbohydrate per 250 g serving, homemade mushroom soup has a low glycemic load of approximately 4.4 and is generally suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when prepared without added sugar or excessive starch thickeners.

cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The elevated sodium content (~993 mg per serving) and moderate saturated fat (~2 g per serving) in cream-based preparations warrant caution for individuals with cardiovascular disease; choosing a broth-based or reduced-cream version and limiting portion size is advisable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Homemade Mushroom Soup is a warm, savoury soup prepared from fresh or dried mushrooms sautéed with onion and garlic in butter or oil, then simmered in vegetable or chicken stock and optionally enriched with cream or evaporated milk. In the Malaysian and Singaporean café context, it is typically served as a starter or light meal, often accompanied by crusty bread or toast. The soup has an earthy, umami-rich flavour with a smooth or lightly chunky texture depending on whether it is blended. Nutritionally, it is relatively low in calories (approximately 53 kcal per 100 g) and carbohydrates (4.6 g per 100 g), making it suitable for low-glycaemic dietary patterns. The estimated glycemic index of 38 places it firmly in the low-GI category. However, sodium content is notable at approximately 397 mg per 100 g, which is clinically relevant for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. Mushrooms contribute B vitamins (riboflavin, niacin, B6), selenium, and potassium. The dish is generally halal and gluten-free when prepared without wheat-based thickeners. It is classified as minimally processed and is a vegetable-group food suitable for most dietary patterns when sodium is controlled.
common_user_queries: Is mushroom soup good for diabetics?
How many calories in mushroom soup?
Is mushroom soup high in sodium?
What is the GI of mushroom soup?
Is mushroom soup suitable for a low-carb diet?
What mushrooms are used in Malaysian mushroom soup?

confidence_reason: No exact HPB Singapore Foodo or IMR Malaysia MyFCD entry for homemade mushroom soup was identified. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy data for cream of mushroom soup prepared with water, which is the closest approved-source match. GI is estimated from analogous vegetable soups in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the adapted nutrient source and analogous GI estimate.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 and is confirmed negligible per USDA data for this preparation. Vitamin B12 is 0.0 as mushroom soup prepared without meat or dairy-derived ingredients contains no meaningful B12; this is confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy data for this food entry. The food is listed under cuisine tag 'Malaysian' from the field survey source; it is served at EC COFFEE BAR - Carpenter Street, Singapore, which is a Malaysian-style coffee bar. Multilingual names follow the Singaporean dish rule (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) given the Singapore serving context and Malaysian cuisine classification. GI of 38 is an analogous estimate; a direct measurement for this specific preparation is not available in the University of Sydney GI Database. Sodium values reflect a standard commercial-style preparation; homemade versions may vary significantly. Price from field survey source: SGD 13.80.

Set gi_category to 'low' consistent with GI = 38 (≤ 55 threshold).
Included all three required health context conditions with specific clinical guidance.
